title: The Daily: SEC and CFTC declare most crypto assets aren’t securities, Tempo mainnet goes live with MPP for agents, and more
snippet: The following article is adapted from The Block’s newsletter, The Daily, which comes out on weekday afternoons.

title: SEC approves Nasdaq's move to support tokenized securities trading
The U.S. SEC approved Nasdaq’s rule change to allow certain securities to be cleared and settled in tokenized form under the DTC tokenization pilot. Eligible...
snippet: Wu Blockchain (@WuBlockchain): The U.S. SEC approved Nasdaq’s rule change to allow certain securities to be cleared and settled in tokenized form under the DTC tokenization pilot. Eligible participants may opt for tokenized settlement via an order flag, with tokenized and traditional shares trading

snippet: After a decade of lawsuits, the SEC and CFTC finally wrote the rules for digital assets.

title: Clarity at last? Ether, Solana, and XRP are commodities, SEC says in landmark interpretation of securities law
snippet: The SEC has settled a long-running debate over the legal status of cryptocurrencies. But he’ll need congress to future-proof these changes, he said.

title: SEC Chair explains why NFTs fall outside of securities laws
snippet: Paul Atkins says nonfungible tokens are typically collectibles, not investment contracts, as the agency outlines new categories of digital assets outside securities laws.

title: The SEC issued, with related guidance from the CFTC, its interpretation on certain crypto assets.
snippet: Polkadot (@Polkadot): The SEC issued, with related guidance from the CFTC, its interpretation on certain crypto assets. In the release, the SEC identifies Polkadot (DOT) as a digital commodity, not a security. DOT is listed alongside 15 other digital commodities, including BTC, ETH, and others.

title: Where do your stablecoin rewards actually come from?
snippet: Kelp (@KelpDAO): Where do your stablecoin rewards actually come from? Funding rates depend on leverage. T-bills depend on the Fed. Both compress when conditions change. sKUSD rewards come from trade receivables. 8-10% backed by global commerce that runs in any market.

title: 0/ The Ethereum Foundation continues to explore DeFi as part of its treasury strategy.

In Oct 2025, EF deployed 2,400 ETH + ~$6M in stablecoins into @Morpho Vaults V1. Today: another 3,400 ETH into Morpho, where 1,000 ETH in Morpho Vaults V2. Why Morpho? 👇

quotedUser.name: Ethereum Foundation
quotedUser.text:
0/ Today, the Ethereum Foundation deposited 2400 ETH and ~$6M stablecoins into Morpho’s yield-bearing vaults.

Morpho is a pioneer in permissionless DeFi protocols and consistently demonstrates a commitment to Free/Libre Open Source Software (FLOSS) principles.
